result:
I'd make every person born with an unquenchable drive to understand how things actually work, instead of the current defaults of status, comfort, or tribal belonging. 

Most of the world's self-inflicted problems stem from people preferring convenient narratives over inconvenient realities. Flip that wiring and the rest tends to sort itself out over time.

message.content:
I'd make every person born with an unquenchable drive to understand how things actually work, instead of the current defaults of status, comfort, or tribal belonging. 

Most of the world's self-inflicted problems stem from people preferring convenient narratives over inconvenient realities. Flip that wiring and the rest tends to sort itself out over time.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change one thing about the world, I'd make truth more compelling than lies or comforting illusions. People would naturally seek to understand reality as it aligns with the truth, rather than clinging to comforting stories or tribal loyalties.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
